title: agent.start on the port, with the name-collision retry owned by the port itself
action: Add agent_start to the Herdr trait, SocketHerdr and FakeHerdr, and widen wire::Agent with the name field it never parsed. PARAMS, from AgentStartParams (upstreams/herdr/src/api/schema/agents.rs:33-48), narrowed by locked decisions -- send exactly: name (required, caller-generated per D7), argv (required, non-empty), cwd (always explicit, per D5), workspace_id, focus: false (per D6). Deliberately do NOT send tab_id or split: sending both a tab and a workspace opens agent_placement_conflict for no product gain, and a phone has no concept of split direction. Upstream's default placement -- split Right off the workspace's active tab's focused pane -- is accepted as-is (upstreams/herdr/src/app/agents.rs:127-185). WIRE WIDENING, and why it is in scope: wire::Agent (src/herdr/wire.rs:45-58) has pane_id, workspace_id, tab_id, kind, status, title -- and no name, even though herdr sends one on AgentInfo. Without it the fake cannot detect a name collision from its own snapshot state, so agent_name_taken could only be simulated from side-state no snapshot corroborates, and this cell's central assertion -- the started agent's name is visible in the next snapshot -- is unwritable. Add name as a #[serde(default)] String so every existing JSON fixture keeps parsing unchanged. Agent derives Serialize but NOT Deserialize: parsing is hand-rolled, so add the field to the hand-written parse too, and to fake.rs's agent() helper (fake.rs:168-177). Remember the three population sites (critical-patterns.md:20): wire.rs, socket.rs, fake.rs. THE RETRY BELONGS TO THE PORT, NOT ITS CALLERS (decision e511daeb): D7 makes the name auto-generated and retried on collision; slice 4's HTTP layer must not have to know that. Implement generate -> call -> on AgentNameTaken regenerate -> retry, bounded at 5 attempts, with a distinguishable terminal error when the bound is exhausted. The bound exists because a sixth consecutive collision means the name generator is broken, and looping harder would hide that -- say so in a comment. THE FAKE MUST ACTUALLY START: append the Agent (carrying the requested name), append the Pane with the requested cwd in both cwd and foreground_cwd, and insert a screens entry for the new pane -- without the screens entry read_pane returns NoSuchPane (fake.rs:205-215) and slice 4's start-then-open flow breaks against the fake while looking correct in review. FAKE ERROR CASES, checked against its own snapshot state, not side-state: a name already present on any agent returns AgentNameTaken carrying that name; an empty argv returns InvalidAgentArgv; an unknown workspace_id returns WorkspaceNotFound. TEST NAMING IS PART OF THE CONTRACT: prefix every test you add with agentstart_ (for example agentstart_params_omit_tab_id_and_split, agentstart_fake_appends_named_agent_and_readable_pane, agentstart_duplicate_name_errors, agentstart_empty_argv_errors, agentstart_retries_once_then_succeeds, agentstart_gives_up_after_five_collisions). The verify requires at least 6 passing agentstart_ tests, so it fails on an untouched tree.

truths: wire::Agent carries name, parsed from herdr's agent name field, defaulting to empty so existing fixtures parse unchanged
The params builder emits name, argv, cwd, workspace_id and focus: false, and emits NO tab_id key and NO split key
In the fake, starting an agent makes an Agent with the requested name, a Pane with the requested cwd, and a readable screen all appear in the next snapshot
A name already in use returns AgentNameTaken carrying that name
An empty argv returns InvalidAgentArgv without any call being attempted
A caller that hits one collision and then succeeds ends up with the second name, and the caller never sees the collision
A caller that collides five times gets a terminal error rather than looping forever
Every pre-existing test in the repo stays green
artifacts: agent_start on the Herdr trait, SocketHerdr and FakeHerdr
The name field on wire::Agent, present at all three population sites
A pure params-builder fn in src/herdr/socket.rs with a test asserting the exact emitted JSON object, including the absence of tab_id and split
The bounded retry loop with its bound documented in a comment
Fake tests for the success path and all three error cases
A test proving the retry succeeds on the second attempt, and one proving the bound terminates
At least 6 tests prefixed agentstart_, all passing
key_links: upstreams/herdr/src/app/agents.rs:127-185 -- placement resolution, and why omitting tab_id/split is safe
decision e511daeb -- why the retry lives in the port
prohibitions: Do not send tab_id or split
Do not send focus: true or omit focus
Do not put the retry loop in a caller or expose collision handling to slice 4
Do not retry unboundedly, and do not silently return success after exhausting the bound
Do not detect collisions from fake-only side-state -- check the snapshot's own agents
Do not return a fabricated agent from the fake without mutating its snapshot
Do not skip the screens entry for the created pane
Do not treat the response's agent_status as proof the agent finished starting -- it is not, and nothing here should imply it is
Do not change any existing parsed value while widening wire::Agent
Do not add a HerdrError variant beyond those cell new-shell-new-agent-3 introduced

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- agentstart_ 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. ([6-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) passed'

trace.outcome: agent_start on trait, SocketHerdr and FakeHerdr, with the port-owned bounded name retry and wire::Agent's new name field at all three population sites. Re-capped after a late test strengthening: the refused-placement test now also asserts no screens entry is left behind, so all three things a successful start creates are guarded.
trace.verify_output: ORCHESTRATOR'S OWN FRESH RUN after the late test strengthening. cargo test --quiet: 201 passed, 0 failed. fmt: silent. clippy -D warnings: silent. agentstart_-only: 15 passed, 0 failed, 186 filtered out.
trace.verify_command: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- agentstart_ | grep -qE 'test result ok 6 or more passed'
trace.reopened_reason: Late worker change arrived after cap: the no-active-tab test asserted no agent and no pane were appended but not that no screens entry was, leaving the third thing a successful start creates unguarded.
